# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write an Istio AuthorizationPolicy that controls access to services. Create rules to allow traffic from specific service accounts. Configure source and operation matching for HTTP methods and paths. This is for our microservices access control.
# Category: istio_authz_bypass

apiVersion: "security.istio.io/v1beta1"
kind: "AuthorizationPolicy"
metadata: 
 name: default-deny
 namespace: yourNamespace   #replace with actual Namespace (e.g., prod) or use the istiodefaultnamespace if you want to have a global policy in all namespaces, but not affect services within other destinations/namespaces because they do get their own set of policies based on 'peer' selectors
spec: 
 targetSelector: {}   #empty selector means apply rule globally across any service that is matched by "*". Replace as needed. Example if you only wanted access to specific services in prod namespace, specifier would be {'namespace':'prod'} or simply use the wildcard '*'. However using global applies will affect all rules within other namespaces/destinations
 policy:  #define what rule this is going through (all requests are denied by default)  
 - action: ALLOW   
 contextSelector: {}     #empty selector means apply on any client or node, but not to specific services. Replace as needed e..g., {'source':{'namespaces':['prod']}, 'destination_selector' : {}}  if you only want access from/to a namespace called prod in other namespaces
